Support.why: O'Connor 2575D fluid head — rated for Alexa Mini LF payload in studio configuration.
100mm bowl tripod legs to pair with the 2575D. Fast deploy on location.

Power.why: V-mount primary. Mini LF draws ~60W body — a Titon 240 runs ~4h depending on accessories.
Overnight-charge V-mount bricks for the next shoot day.

Media.why: Required — Mini LF records to Codex Compact Drive. One 2TB drive fits ~45 min of 4.5K ARRIRAW.

Monitoring.why: Wireless video village to director and DIT. 4K support for native Mini LF output.
Director monitor with integrated Bolt 500 receiver — pairs to the Bolt 4K TX.

Handheld Rig.why: Master Grip Zoom — wired zoom and record control for handheld / Steadicam.
15mm studio rods for accessory mounting (matte box, follow focus, EVF arm).

Support.why: 2575D handles the V-Raptor's weight when rigged out.
100mm bowl legs to pair with the 2575D.

Power.why: V-mount V-Raptor plate required. Draws ~75W; plan two Titon 240s per camera per day.
Four-channel V-mount charger for overnight turnaround.

Media.why: Required — RED PRO CFexpress 660GB cards. Approved for 8K VV recording.

Monitoring.why: Wireless 4K video village for director and script.
Director monitor with built-in Bolt 500 RX, pairs to the Bolt 4K TX.

Handheld Rig.why: 15mm studio rods for matte box, follow focus, and battery plate mounting.

Support.why: Flowtech 100 legs — fast deploy for documentary work.

Power.why: V-mount adapter plate carries a Titon 240 — more runtime than BP-U batteries.
Overnight V-mount charger if you carry V-mount plates.

Media.why: FX6 uses CFexpress Type A — this is a different physical card. Substitute with Sony CFexpress Type A cards from the rental house (placeholder card shown here).

Monitoring.why: Director monitor for talent-facing or video-village setups.

Handheld Rig.why: 15mm rods for matte box and follow focus on the FX6 cage.
